textByHash.015978729d5508f9377e7caecb9d8bc63a47ed95: Oh well, I'll see if I can find someone else instead.
textByHash.0ceb9c6f8ea962cc9dfe01eaef0604e27859954f: Just have patience, my young friend! Have a wander around town, see if anyone has jobs for you!
textByHash.0d9ca81f40185848ddca3a10abf55c96c4bd3479: That's a little clumsy! Here, I've got a copy, please deliver this one instead.
textByHash.166c2b9ba49f854ccc2b08a6b51ff786f507fb3f: Wonderful, that's just wonderful! Here, have these two seeds as a reward. They may not be Kelda hop seeds, but you should be able to grow a decent amount of herbs with them.
textByHash.219144abb5930d189a07e7cc0f7139e487febfe4: What, with the wars raging up there?
textByHash.31db08477c15f1b5087f4342c2bf11a11b5bec56: We dwarves went underground a long time ago, see. For a while the city of Keldagrim was completely cut off from the rest of Gielinor simply waiting out the wars of the humans and the gods.
textByHash.3922c1488f23f57cd2ab1e2e1b59d341519d3241: Hello again! Have you been able to deliver that letter yet?
textByHash.3f9cb3dee24d866b3e3883da949e1f715f6811f7: Happy to hear that! His name is Elstan, he lives just south of Falador.
textByHash.49267d7f674d12b5447827e3d4798677b9324464: Yes and no. Yes, you do have to wait, but no, you don't have to stay here. You can help out people in the city, for instance, there's always someone who needs help with an urgent job.
textByHash.49ffb05076d94a2f96ec7cadad7fd63ead42350f: Oh, there's one in Keldagrim-East, upstairs in the Laughing Miner. They brew their own beer they do.
textByHash.4f3461cf4149734c55088ac6b51ecb3de21b0d21: Very well. That can be quite relaxing, actually, I often do it myself.
textByHash.50c859a26ab985590c0839f1d8d37892946a890e: There is a patch in the palace garden that you're more than welcome to use.
textByHash.5162af9fcdbc7c40cc940635fbd9ec679595d661: In the Era of Kings, many centuries ago, it was harder of course. Our steam technology was not developed as far yet and getting fresh supplies of food was always a problem.
textByHash.53aaac8610f5c6c273d00204a2ded8304f650c77: No no, just people like shopkeepers and such, they're quite willing to teach you things in exchange for the jobs as well.
textByHash.53b933a3bcac6ffa01a2ad92364e39c0c7bd3d0a: No no, not quite. But I do look after it. It's the palace garden, you see. I keep it tidy, keep it living.
textByHash.5d5e3f26c0091b374fece760b8a1c43ea0fc6cf0: How are you getting along with growing your seeds into hops?
textByHash.65141caea4bc8cddab23bc6e016bd22715889f47: Only when King Alvis heroically defended the city against an invasion of trolls did we dare to venture out and see how much the world had changed.
textByHash.6c2ad73f1f7d32ca6b62f67bc208bb1215ec58de: Just get rid of the weeds in the patch, plant you seeds and wait for the hops to grow. The miracle of nature will do the rest!
textByHash.6fe9fa465059b137011ca1b4db415b01f2b52c9f: Oh, I'm not boring you, am I?
textByHash.71b55a7305aa6df8c37b2b6ef53e8844ee7570a0: This made it hard, though, to determine when the wars above the ground were over. Perhaps Keldagrim had remained hidden for far too long.
textByHash.7c2c402abe1685653958b9677e0332a275de303e: Yes? What can a humble gardener like myself help you with?
textByHash.80316b7983bdc4e338eb03361589785965fd994b: You will have to bring your hops to a brewery.
textByHash.8fac09c0cf8478b9d218c76b0519332f28b36fd1: But here, tell you what, I'll give you some seeds if you can deliver this letter to a friend in Falador.
textByHash.95128d27bbff07f98c5e13570be9833f39be7a1b: Ah! Fortunately I found some hops after your recent harvest. Here, I believe this belongs to you.
textByHash.a93f72899ab9eee8c225624e3cf4a73eecf98444: Ah well, enough history lessons I should say. I must really get back to my gardening.
textByHash.b55b3cb1eb570c16265aa7d15211f55e7c0b1e7d: Marvelous, those seeds are quite rare don't you know!
textByHash.c6a358f434344938926b8088f687e7f86541cbba: We can grow a little food underground, but most of it comes from the extensive steamcart network that runs beneath the earth.
textByHash.c90acfd81684c62fe8778c7c372655d219cfce8e: Now now, my young human friend! Are you sure about that?
textByHash.cbb4ff8f858c2eebde6a9f64bb4221ed2fe02166: Oh no, you don't have to do it all, I just thought you wanted something to do while you were waiting.
textByHash.d0cb288d00dd8679a8e87fe9e53aa7a31f8572c1: Oh yes, as I was saying, it was difficult to make a decent living here. I'm afraid this garden was rather neglected during that time.
textByHash.d814bb10b500e81c7e7444096058e60ae8bd611e: Oh, I'm sad to hear that.
textByHash.dc20a0d49f2b3d533ad3499bdfebc580cca3e35e: Perhaps you should go back to the brewery, I wouldn't be surprised if your stout has fermented by now.
textByHash.e25963ff7001154ff4413766254285ccb6541cd5: It's hard to grow much on these hard rocks deep beneath these mountains, you know.
textByHash.e42e5d4d4cfabcc733df233f31bd2166fa93218e: Just take care of the weed in the patch and plant your seeds. They'll grow into hops very quickly, if you're lucky. Kelda seeds have a remarkably fast grow rate.
textByHash.e497a085c24b2224481bed9079c14848b32dbac9: [laugh], it doesn't quite work like that, I'm afraid!
textByHash.ec12a829940e2a129087958a8cc85d791ecb01b4: I suppose it doesn't, really, it's just the mumblings of an old dwarf.
textByHash.ee8361c081517f89868224086858e6ace0809563: I'm sure someone there will be able to help you out, it's not my specialty you see.
textByHash.f72019ce04abebda6f97705575a0035551fd75c6: Come to visit my garden then, have you, human?
textByHash.ff8cf10a3b4e717fa8f69437047117eaa2c2d204: First of all, you must remember that these seeds only grow underground. Sunlight is harmful to them, for some strange reason.
